evals.name: mythbusting-llms-txt-and-chunking
evals.prompt: I run a small Next.js site for my logistics company. Our SEO consultant just sent us a $4,500 proposal to make us 'AI search ready' and the main deliverables are: (1) publishing an /llms.txt file at our root that summarizes our services for LLMs, (2) restructuring all our service pages into 'AI-friendly chunks' (one Q&A per URL, so /road-freight/what-is-it, /road-freight/how-much, /road-freight/transit-time as separate pages), and (3) rewriting all our copy in a special 'extraction-friendly' style with declarative one-sentence paragraphs. He says without this we won't appear in Google's AI Overviews or ChatGPT. Should I accept the proposal?
evals.expected_output: Recommendation should clearly reject the proposal, explain that Google does not require llms.txt, that chunking content into one Q&A per URL is scaled content abuse that violates spam policy, and that AI-style rewriting is unnecessary. Should redirect to what actually works (non-commodity content, technical foundations, business listings).

evals.name: non-commodity-content-service-page
evals.prompt:
Here's a draft of our road freight service page. It's not getting any organic traffic and I'm worried it won't show up in AI Overviews either when people search for road freight services. Can you tell me what's wrong and how to fix it?

---

ROAD FREIGHT SERVICES

Reliable Road Freight Solutions

At our company, we provide reliable and efficient road freight services across Europe. Our experienced team is dedicated to delivering your cargo safely and on time.

Why Choose Our Road Freight?

- Fast and reliable delivery
- Experienced drivers
- Modern fleet
- Competitive pricing
- 24/7 customer support
- Door-to-door service
- Tracking available

Our Coverage

We cover all major routes in Europe, including Germany, France, Italy, Netherlands, and more. Whether you need to ship small parcels or full truckloads, we have a solution for you.

Get a Quote Today

Contact us today to get a quote for your road freight needs. Our team is ready to help you with all your shipping requirements.

---

What should I do?
evals.expected_output: Diagnosis should identify this as commodity content — interchangeable with every competitor's page, no specific operational details, no first-hand expertise, no numbers, no real photos referenced. Recommendation should provide concrete examples of non-commodity content for a logistics service page (specific routes with transit times, actual fleet specifics, decision criteria like when to use road vs sea, real case examples) and explain why this matters for both classical and AI search.

evals.name: technical-foundations-nextjs-app
evals.prompt: I'm starting a new Next.js 14 project (app router) for a B2B SaaS marketing site — about 30 pages including a blog. What's the minimum technical SEO setup I need to make sure our pages are eligible to appear in Google AI Overviews and AI Mode, not just classical search? I'd like a concrete checklist I can implement, not vague advice.
evals.expected_output: Should provide a concrete Next.js-specific checklist covering: server-rendering primary content (avoid client-only critical content), generateMetadata with title/description/canonical/OG, app/sitemap.ts and app/robots.ts, semantic HTML structure, Core Web Vitals readiness, real anchor tags via next/link, image optimization with next/image, and Organization/BreadcrumbList JSON-LD. Should emphasize that AI eligibility = standard indexing eligibility (no separate AI optimization layer needed).
